why_tempus: Dr. Rahman requested a broader liquid-biopsy option for patients when tissue is scarce. Tempus offers both the 105-gene xF panel and the 523-gene xF+ panel to support her clinical needs.
supporting_crm_excerpt: She asked whether Tempus offers a broader liquid-biopsy option beyond a focused panel.

product_snapshot.display_text: xF is a 105-gene liquid-biopsy ctDNA panel that detects SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements.
xF+ is a 523-gene liquid-biopsy panel covering SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements.
xF results are typically expected within 7 days of specimen retrieval.
xF+ results are typically expected within 7 to 9 days of specimen retrieval.

objection_response.text: Tempus provides two distinct liquid-biopsy options to address your concern regarding panel breadth. The xF panel is a 105-gene liquid-biopsy ctDNA panel, while the xF+ panel is a 523-gene liquid-biopsy panel. Both panels cover SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements. Results for xF are typically expected within 7 days of specimen retrieval, and xF+ results are typically expected within 7 to 9 days of specimen retrieval.

meeting_script.text: Dr. Rahman, thank you for sharing your interest in broader liquid-biopsy options for your breast cancer patients. To address this, Tempus offers the 105-gene xF panel and the 523-gene xF+ panel. Both options provide comprehensive coverage for SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements. Results for xF are typically expected within 7 days of specimen retrieval, while xF+ results are typically expected within 7 to 9 days. Would you like to review the xF+ panel specifications today?
